  "text": "The @ConfigKey and @ConfigDef annotation pattern in Apache Kafka refers to a configuration documentation system based on the ConfigDef class and its inner ConfigKey class. The pattern consists of three main components:\n\n1. **Annotation Definition**: ConfigDef (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/ConfigDef.java) is the core class that defines configuration specifications. It contains a static inner class ConfigKey that represents a configuration entry with metadata including name, type, documentation, default value, validators, importance level, and other configuration properties.\n\n2. **AbstractConfig Base Class**: AbstractConfig (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/AbstractConfig.java) is the base class that all configuration classes extend. It provides parsing and validation functionality using the ConfigDef specification.\n\n3. **Configuration Subclasses**: Over 80 classes extend AbstractConfig and define a static CONFIG field of type ConfigDef. These include:\n   - Client configs: AdminClientConfig, ConsumerConfig, ProducerConfig\n   - Connect configs: ConverterConfig, ConnectorConfig, TaskConfig, WorkerConfig, and various transform and predicate configs\n   - Server configs: ServerConfigs, SocketServerConfigs, KRaftConfigs, ReplicationConfigs\n   - Coordinator configs: GroupCoordinatorConfig, ShareCoordinatorConfig, TransactionStateManagerConfig\n   - Storage configs: LogConfig, RemoteLogManagerConfig, CleanerConfig\n   - Streams configs: StreamsConfig, TopologyConfig\n   - And 40+ more configuration classes\n\n4. **Documentation Generators**: PredicateDoc (connect/runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/connect/tools/PredicateDoc.java) and TransformationDoc (connect/runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/connect/tools/TransformationDoc.java) are utilities that use reflection to extract ConfigDef and documentation from transformation and predicate classes to generate configuration documentation.\n\n5. **Schema Utilities**: KafkaConfigSchema (metadata/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/metadata/KafkaConfigSchema.java) provides schema information about configuration keys, translating between ConfigDef.Type and ConfigEntry.ConfigType.\n\nThe pattern allows Kafka to maintain self-documenting configuration with type information, validation, and dependency management across the entire codebase."

  "text": "The @ConfigKey and @ConfigDef annotation pattern in Apache Kafka refers to a configuration documentation system based on the ConfigDef class and its inner ConfigKey class. The pattern consists of three main components:\n\n1. **Annotation Definition**: ConfigDef (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/ConfigDef.java) is the core class that defines configuration specifications. It contains a static inner class ConfigKey that represents a configuration entry with metadata including name, type, documentation, default value, validators, importance level, and other configuration properties.\n\n2. **AbstractConfig Base Class**: AbstractConfig (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/AbstractConfig.java) is the base class that all configuration classes extend. It provides parsing and validation functionality using the ConfigDef specification.\n\n3. **Configuration Subclasses**: Over 80 classes extend AbstractConfig and define a static CONFIG field of type ConfigDef. These include:\n   - Client configs: AdminClientConfig, ConsumerConfig, ProducerConfig\n   - Connect configs: ConverterConfig, ConnectorConfig, TaskConfig, WorkerConfig, and various transform and predicate configs\n   - Server configs: ServerConfigs, SocketServerConfigs, KRaftConfigs, ReplicationConfigs\n   - Coordinator configs: GroupCoordinatorConfig, ShareCoordinatorConfig, TransactionStateManagerConfig\n   - Storage configs: LogConfig, RemoteLogManagerConfig, CleanerConfig\n   - Streams configs: StreamsConfig, TopologyConfig\n   - And 40+ more configuration classes\n\n4. **Documentation Generators**: PredicateDoc (connect/runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/connect/tools/PredicateDoc.java) and TransformationDoc (connect/runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/connect/tools/TransformationDoc.java) are utilities that use reflection to extract ConfigDef and documentation from transformation and predicate classes to generate configuration documentation.\n\n5. **Schema Utilities**: KafkaConfigSchema (metadata/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/metadata/KafkaConfigSchema.java) provides schema information about configuration keys, translating between ConfigDef.Type and ConfigEntry.ConfigType.\n\nThe pattern allows Kafka to maintain self-documenting configuration with type information, validation, and dependency management across the entire codebase."

    89→  "text": "The @ConfigKey and @ConfigDef annotation pattern in Apache Kafka refers to a configuration documentation system based on the ConfigDef class and its inner ConfigKey class. The pattern consists of three main components:\n\n1. **Annotation Definition**: ConfigDef (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/ConfigDef.java) is the core class that defines configuration specifications. It contains a static inner class ConfigKey that represents a configuration entry with metadata including name, type, documentation, default value, validators, importance level, and other configuration properties.\n\n2. **AbstractConfig Base Class**: AbstractConfig (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/AbstractConfig.java) is the base class that all configuration classes extend. It provides parsing and validation functionality using the ConfigDef specification.\n\n3. **Configuration Subclasses**: Over 80 classes extend AbstractConfig and define a static CONFIG field of type ConfigDef. These include:\n   - Client configs: AdminClientConfig, ConsumerConfig, ProducerConfig\n   - Connect configs: ConverterConfig, ConnectorConfig, TaskConfig, WorkerConfig, and various transform and predicate configs\n   - Server configs: ServerConfigs, SocketServerConfigs, KRaftConfigs, ReplicationConfigs\n   - Coordinator configs: GroupCoordinatorConfig, ShareCoordinatorConfig, TransactionStateManagerConfig\n   - Storage configs: LogConfig, RemoteLogManagerConfig, CleanerConfig\n   - Streams configs: StreamsConfig, TopologyConfig\n   - And 40+ more configuration classes\n\n4. **Documentation Generators**: PredicateDoc (connect/runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/connect/tools/PredicateDoc.java) and TransformationDoc (connect/runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/connect/tools/TransformationDoc.java) are utilities that use reflection to extract ConfigDef and documentation from transformation and predicate classes to generate configuration documentation.\n\n5. **Schema Utilities**: KafkaConfigSchema (metadata/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/metadata/KafkaConfigSchema.java) provides schema information about configuration keys, translating between ConfigDef.Type and ConfigEntry.ConfigType.\n\nThe pattern allows Kafka to maintain self-documenting configuration with type information, validation, and dependency management across the entire codebase."
